message:
feat(core): add input_reserve S3-FIFO admission queue and semantic_anomaly EWMA detector

Two new core modules for latency immunity under remote mux degradation:

input_reserve.rs (1379 lines):
- S3-FIFO admission queue ensuring interactive input (keystrokes, viewport
  reflow) is never starved by background mux state synchronization
- ReserveFloorPolicy: configurable minimum budget that must remain available
  for local-interactive work after all reservation accounting
- ShedMarker for tagging items that exceed admission thresholds
- InputReserveController: top-level coordinator integrating queue, policy,
  and shed decisions into the resize scheduler pipeline

semantic_anomaly.rs (260 lines):
- EWMA-based detector for sudden semantic shifts in terminal output streams
- Computes Z-scores over rolling embedding-vector statistics to flag
  context collapse and hallucination loops without requiring LLM calls
- Zero-shot guardrail: operates purely on statistical divergence

Also registers both modules in lib.rs and adds proptest_input_reserve.rs
(708 lines) with property-based tests covering queue admission, floor
clamping, shed marker propagation, and edge cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
